title: Wood Mantel with Hidden Storage
summary: A floating mantel that's actually a shallow box with a concealed-hinge front — remotes and cords disappear behind what looks like solid wood.
description:
From the front, this reads as a solid wood mantel shelf. In reality it's a five-sided box — top, bottom, two ends, and a center divider for rigidity — with the front panel mounted on concealed surface hinges instead of screwed on permanently, so it swings open to hide a remote, cable box, or whatever else you don't want visible near a TV or fireplace.

Install the hinges and test the door's swing before the box goes on the wall — once it's hanging, you're reaching up and inside a dark cavity to adjust a hinge, which is a genuinely awkward way to fine-tune a fit that's much easier to get right on a bench. The example below is sized for a 60" mantel; the source calls for the top, bottom, and front to be cut to your own desired length minus 1-1/2" for the top and bottom, so treat every length here as a stand-in for your actual measurement.
timeLabel: 4–6 hrs for a mantel around 60" long — scales with length

materials.name: 1x6 board (roughly 3x your mantel length, plus a foot)
1x3 board, cut to fit for hanging cleats

cutList.note: Cut to your desired mantel length minus 1-1/2".
Cut to your desired mantel length.
Measure and cut to fit inside your assembled box.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Attach the two ends and the center divider to the bottom board with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails. Orient the grain of the ends so it runs horizontal — this is a small detail but it reads noticeably more finished than end-grain showing vertically.
materials: 1x6 board (roughly 3x your mantel length, plus a foot)

steps.title: Hang the front on hinges now
steps.body: Install the concealed surface-mount hinges and mount the front so it's flush on the bottom and sides — do this before the top goes on, while you can still reach inside easily. Test the swing and adjust the hinge position until the fit is right; this gets much harder to do once the box is closed up and on the wall.
materials: 1x6 board (roughly 3x your mantel length, plus a foot)

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top board to the ends and center divider — do not attach it to the front door, which needs to swing independently.
materials: 1x6 board (roughly 3x your mantel length, plus a foot)

steps.title: Add hanging cleats and mount
steps.body: Measure and cut the 1x3 cleats to fit inside the box, then nail and glue them in place. Hang the mantel to wall studs using the 2-1/2" screws, checking with a level as you go — a mantel that isn't dead level is one of the more obvious mistakes on a piece this visible in a room.
materials: 1x3 board, cut to fit for hanging cleats

images.alt: Wall-mounted wood mantel with a concealed hinged front for hidden storage
